President Donald Trump holds his 9th cabinet meeting at the White House.

byMatthew Seeman

LAS VEGAS (KSNV) —Olympic medal-winning swimmer Katie Grimes felt the love upon returning home to Las Vegas.

Grimes landed at Harry Reid International Airport to a surprise welcome from family, friends and supporters Tuesday night.

People held signs reading "Welcome home Katie!" and "You've Got Silver Swagger" to congratulate Grimes, who returned from Parisafter winning the silver medalin the 400-meter medley.

KSNV

She even showed off the medal to attendees to celebrate her Olympic accomplishment.

Talking to reporters, Grimes said she appreciated all of the support she received from Las Vegas.

The experience in Paris was very different from her first Summer Games in Tokyo in 2021, when she was just 15 and traveling abroad for the first time, Grimes told reporters.

"To walk away with a medal, I'm stoked on that. But in LA, I'm definitely looking for bigger things," she said, looking ahead to the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.

Grimes said she's eager to get back to training as soon as possible, but she will be sure to enjoy some time off right now.

Nevada State Bank, one of Grimes' sponsors, helped organize the welcome.

2025Sinclair, Inc.
title: Olympic medalist Katie Grimes gets big welcome home in return to Las Vegas
